items.title: Slot: Liverpool unlikely to make any signings in Jan
items.summary: Arne Slot has admitted he doesn't expect Liverpool to make any signings in the January transfer window but said the club will always look for opportunities in the market.

items.title: Transfer window latest, Emery shrugs off Tielemans row, and more: football – live
items.summary: ⚽ The latest football news heading into the weekend ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for | Mail Dominic This weekend’s Premier League games are the reverse fixtures of the season openers, back in sun-soaked August. Looking back, the most jarring result was Nottingham Forest 3-1 Brentford, with the teams taking very different trajectories since then. “It was certainly a tough day for us all, but it is pretty obvious to see the development that we have made in the months since that, and I am delighted with the progress we have made,” Brentford manager Keith Andrews said yesterday. Continue reading...

items.title: Een zak geld kan wél scoren: Engeland extreem dominant in Europa
items.summary: Johan Cruijff zei ooit dat hij een zak geld nooit had zien scoren. Waarheid als een koe, maar dat financiële middelen wel degelijk kunnen helpen om goed te presteren, bewijzen de Premier League-clubs dit seizoen. Het Engelse voetbal is opvallend dominant in de Europese competities.

items.title: Emmanuel van de Blaak: 'Almere City moet voor de derde plek gaan'
items.summary: Emmanuel van de Blaak (20) is na jaren voor Jong PSV te hebben gespeeld sinds dit seizoen centrumverdediger bij Almere City. Het bekeravontuur van de Flevolanders eindigde afgelopen week tegen Telstar, de ploeg van trainer Jeroen Rijsdijk kan zich nu op de competitie richten waarin plaatsing voor de play-offs afgedwongen moet worden. ‘Best of the rest is een mooie doelstelling.’

items.title: Rodman contract saga ends with record Spirit deal
items.summary: United States forward Trinity Rodman signed a record three-year contract with the Washington Spirit on Thursday to remain at the club, ending months-long uncertainty over the star's future.

items.title: Casemiro to leave Man United at end of the season
items.summary: Casemiro will leave Manchester United when his contract expires at the end of the season, the club have confirmed.

items.title: Dual-nationals help Mexico snap winless streak
items.summary: Dual-nationals Richard Ledezma and Brian Gutierrez earned starts on Thursday as Mexico claimed a victory over Panama.

items.title: Premier League Power Rankings: Rating all 20 teams on performances, not results
items.summary: In the debut of ESPN's Premier League Power Rankings: a look at all 20 teams, including why Liverpool, Chelsea and Tottenham are better than the table suggests.

items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Slot lovend over Frimpong: 'Hij heeft veel invloed op Liverpool'
items.summary: Jeremie Frimpong is los bij Liverpool. De international van Oranje lijkt zijn basisplaats te hebben veroverd. Op bezoek bij Marseille (0-3) blonk de vleugelspeler uit , tot tevredenheid van Arne Slot.

items.title: Officieel: Szymanski ruilt Fenerbahçe in voor Ligue 1
items.summary: Sebastian Szymanski heeft een nieuwe club gevonden. De oud-Feyenoorder heeft Fenerbahçe verruild voor Stade Rennes. De Franse club legt naar verluidt om en nabij tien miljoen euro voor hem op tafel.

items.title: 'A DIFFERENT Ferrari!' | Hamilton takes NEW SF-26 for a spin in Italy
items.summary: See the first pictures of Lewis Hamilton taking the new SF-26 Ferrari around the Fiorano test track.

items.title: Wolves reject Leeds bid for striker Strand Larsen
items.summary: Leeds United fail with a bid to sign Wolves striker Jorgen Strand Larsen.

items.title: Rodman 'becomes highest-paid women's footballer'
items.summary: Trinity Rodman signs a new three-year deal with Washington Spirit that will reportedly make her the world's highest-paid female footballer.

items.title: Football transfer rumours: Palmer to Manchester United? Vinícius Jr set for Saudi move?
items.summary: Today’s rumours have a northern feel Casemiro’s decision to leave Manchester United when his contract expires at the end of the season will intensify the club’s search for a midfielder. The Brazilian’s exit will also knock £350k a week off their outgoings, which should bring a smile to Sir Jim Ratcliffe as United look to follow up last week’s derby win when they travel to … oh … Arsenal this weekend. Not exactly like for like but stories are circulating that Wythenshawe lad Cole Palmer is homesick at Chelsea and pondering a return to Manchester this summer. The twist is that he wouldn’t be heading back to City as Palmer prefers the red of United – the club he, wait for it, supported as a boy. United scouts were also reportedly in Spain last weekend to discuss on-loan Marcus Rashford’s future and check out Real Sociedad’s versatile midfielder/forward Mikel Oyarzabal. Fun fact: both Palmer (equaliser) and Oyarzabal (winner) scored in the final of Euro 2024. Continue reading...

items.title: Man Utd keen to resume Mainoo contract talks
items.summary: Manchester United are keen to resume talks with midfielder Kobbie Mainoo over a new contract that would bring him in line with his fellow senior squad members.

items.title: Wolves reject Leeds bid for Strand Larsen
items.summary: Wolves have rejected an initial bid from Leeds for Jorgen Strand Larsen.

items.title: Poker player’s punt on Wednesday shrouded in secrecy after Blades missteps
items.summary: James Bord’s consortium is the preferred bidder to take over Sheffield Wednesday but his data-led player recruitment record is mixed, especially with United Sheffield Wednesday fans will be delighted to hear that one associate of James Bord describes the preferred bidder for their club as “a mini Tony Bloom”, although the professional poker player’s references from the other side of the Steel City are rather less complimentary. Until it became clear late last year that Bord was planning to buy Wednesday his data company, Short Circuit Science, had a consultancy contract with Sheffield United to assist with their recruitment, which, as their position in the lower reaches of the Championship indicates, has delivered limited success. Continue reading...

This time it is the Rennes centre-back Jérémy Jacquet, who would offer something the team is lacking. Continue reading...

items.title: Infantino makes joke about British football fans - but it's factually incorrect
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino made a jibe at British football fans while defending the upcoming World Cup and its ticket prices.
